summary: Follow-up review queue created while adding rule-spec candidate evaluation and Python function budget lint candidates.

observations.problem: The new non-blocking Python function budget pass now surfaces existing long, high-parameter, or high-branch functions across ops, tools, and tests.
observations.why_it_matters: The budget signal is useful only if maintainers periodically triage the candidate queue and decide which hotspots should be split, exempted, or tracked as accepted debt.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a follow-up report or mutation-proposal adapter that groups python_function_budget_candidate findings by profile and budget type, then opens focused refactor proposals for the highest-impact runtime surfaces.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-10 KST: `make function-budget-refactor-proposals` now consumes the classified wiki_lint queue, preserves symbol/line/profile/budget metrics, groups the current 83 function-budget candidates by owner and scope, separates large main functions and shared runtime helpers, and emits only bounded small adapter refactor proposals to `ops/reports/function-budget-refactor-proposals.json`.

observations.problem: release_smoke packages the deterministic manifest inventory while live corpus source traces still reference excluded external-reports/ and tmp/ paths, so unpacked release lint/eval can fail even when the live vault gates pass.
observations.why_it_matters: Release packaging should prove packaged-copy replayability; source traces to excluded local-only artifacts make release readiness depend on files that are intentionally absent from the package.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep release-smoke regression coverage for source traces pointing at package-excluded prefixes so packaged-copy replayability does not drift silently.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-19 by excluding `external-reports/` and `tmp/` from the deterministic release manifest, removing release-excluded external report paths from corpus Source trace sections, and replacing the `tmp/` Source trace entry with stable ops runtime evidence.
